claim: Cognition engineers have shifted from using AI in IDEs to giving tasks to autonomous agents and trusting them to perform, then reviewing code and testing locally
actionable: Transition from AI-as-IDE-copilot to AI-as-agent workflows by architecting features locally then delegating implementation to autonomous agents
source_title: How We Build: Cognition Engineers on the Frontier of AI with Grant Sanderson

claim: With multiple autonomous agent sessions running in parallel, the challenge becomes managing and reviewing multiple concurrent sessions rather than keeping human brain parallelized
actionable: Develop session management practices and dashboards to track multiple parallel agents; human attention becomes the bottleneck, not agent capability
source_title: How We Build: Cognition Engineers on the Frontier of AI with Grant Sanderson

claim: At Cognition, engineers have broken down software engineering into architecting features and building features, with humans now only doing architecture while agents handle building
actionable: Focus your coding work on high-level architecture and design; delegate feature implementation details to autonomous agents
source_title: How We Build: Cognition Engineers on the Frontier of AI with Grant Sanderson

claim: Creating beautiful code and architectural taste is a quality unique to hiring at Cognition that distinguishes between solving problems and creating elegant solutions
actionable: When reviewing agent-generated code, prioritize solutions that are not just correct but also demonstrate good taste and elegance
source_title: How We Build: Cognition Engineers on the Frontier of AI with Grant Sanderson
